subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Help the Reading Ranger complete the Story Sequence: Beginning, Middle, End trail.
lesson.objective: I can practice story sequence: beginning, middle, end with careful listening, reading, or writing.
lesson.concept_explanation: This mission teaches story sequence: beginning, middle, end using child-friendly models and short practice steps.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may answer from a first clue instead of checking the whole word, sentence, or story.
lesson.real_world_connection: Readers and writers use this skill in books, signs, notes, and classroom talk.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read or listen carefully, use the model, then choose or write the answer.
lesson.mini_lesson: First study the model. Then say the sounds or words slowly. Last, check that your answer matches the prompt.

worked_examples.text: Watch: Use the story_sequence model, name the important part, then answer.
Demo: Try one with help before independent practice.

question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.correct_answer: Pam got a seed. | Pam planted the seed. | A flower grew.
hints: Say the word slowly.
Listen for the target sound or word part.
Check your answer with the model.
questions.explanation: The answer is Pam got a seed. | Pam planted the seed. | A flower grew..
questions.feedback: Yes — Pam got a seed. | Pam planted the seed. | A flower grew. fits.
choices: Pam got a seed. | Pam planted the seed. | A flower grew.

Pam planted the seed. | Pam got a seed. | A flower grew.
